description: The system encountered an error where a lower-level driver tried to access memory at a higher-level interrupt request level (IRQL), causing an IRQL not less than or equal to exception.
fix: Restart your PC, then update or roll back any recently installed drivers.
Use BlueScreenView or similar tools to identify the problematic driver and contact its manufacturer for updates.

description: A kernel mode exception occurred, indicating a serious error in device drivers or system software.
fix: Restart your PC and undo any recent driver or software installs that might be causing the issue.
Use BlueScreenView to identify the problematic driver and update it from the manufacturer's website.

description: An error occurred while accessing or reading data from an NTFS volume.
fix: Run 'chkdsk /f' on the affected drive to scan for and repair disk errors, bad sectors, and other issues.
Check if the affected drive is listed as healthy in Disk Management; if not, replace it.

description: A data bus error occurred while accessing memory or hardware components, often caused by faulty RAM.
fix: Check your RAM by running Windows Memory Diagnostic and reseating the memory sticks.
Use a third-party tool like MemTest86 to test your RAM for errors.

description: A system service encountered an unexpected error and crashed, usually caused by faulty drivers or software.
fix: Update your graphics and system drivers to the latest version from their respective manufacturers' websites.
Use BlueScreenView to identify the problematic driver and update it.

description: The system encountered a page fault error while trying to access memory that is not paged out, often caused by hardware issues or faulty drivers.
fix: Run Windows Memory Diagnostic to test your RAM for faults and physical errors.
Check for overheating components and update any problematic drivers.

description: Windows cannot access the system partition during startup, typically caused by a corrupted or missing boot configuration or changed SATA/AHCI settings.
fix: Boot from Windows installation media and use Startup Repair.
Check BIOS/UEFI settings to ensure the correct boot drive is selected and SATA mode hasn't changed.

description: A system thread generated an exception that the error handler did not catch, often pointing to a faulty driver.
fix: Boot into Safe Mode and uninstall recently added drivers or software.
Run 'sfc /scannow' to repair corrupted system files.

description: The CPU generated a trap the kernel failed to catch, commonly tied to hardware failure or overclocking instability.
fix: Reset any overclocking settings in BIOS to default values.
Test RAM with MemTest86 and check CPU temperatures under load.

description: A Non-Maskable Interrupt hardware failure occurred, usually indicating a serious hardware problem.
fix: Run hardware diagnostics for CPU, RAM, and motherboard.
Check event logs for hardware errors and consider professional hardware inspection.

description: A kernel-mode process or driver attempted to perform an illegal memory allocation.
fix: Update or uninstall recently installed drivers, particularly network or storage drivers.
Use Driver Verifier to isolate the problematic driver.

issue: Driver IRQL Not Less or Equal
description: A driver accessed memory at an incorrect IRQL level.
fix: Identify and update the driver listed in the BSOD minidump file.
Run Windows Update and check for driver updates from your hardware manufacturer.

description: A critical system process, such as winlogon.exe or csrss.exe, terminated unexpectedly.
fix: Run 'sfc /scannow' and 'DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth' from an elevated command prompt.
Check for malware using Windows Defender or a trusted third-party tool.

description: A critical system object, such as a process or thread, was unexpectedly terminated.
fix: Run 'chkdsk /r' to check the storage drive for errors.
Test RAM with MemTest86 and update storage drivers.

description: A driver is stuck in an inconsistent or invalid power state, commonly triggered during sleep/wake cycles.
fix: Update all drivers, especially USB and network adapters.
Disable fast startup via Power Options and test if the issue recurs.

description: The GPU driver failed to respond in time, causing the display driver to reset. Common with AMD and Nvidia GPUs.
fix: Perform a clean install of the latest GPU driver using DDU (Display Driver Uninstaller).
Check GPU temperatures and reseat the graphics card if applicable.

description: A Windows Driver Framework violation occurred, often related to a third-party driver not following WDF rules.
fix: Use Windows Driver Verifier to identify the offending driver.
Update or reinstall drivers from the hardware manufacturer's official site.

description: A Deferred Procedure Call watchdog timeout occurred. Often caused by outdated SSD firmware or incompatible drivers.
fix: Update SSD firmware and storage controller drivers.
Check for Windows updates and update all device drivers.

description: A critical user-mode subsystem process, such as Winlogon or CSRSS, terminated unexpectedly.
fix: Boot from Windows installation media and run Startup Repair.
Restore the system using System Restore to a point before the issue began.

description: An application or service was denied access to a resource, usually due to insufficient permissions.
fix: Run the affected application as Administrator.
Check file/folder permissions and ensure your user account has the appropriate rights.

description: The system ran out of available memory to complete the requested operation.
fix: Close unnecessary background applications to free up RAM.
Increase the virtual memory (page file) size via System Properties > Advanced > Performance Settings.

description: An invalid parameter was passed to a function or service, often triggered by corrupt system files or registry entries.
fix: Run 'sfc /scannow' to check for and repair corrupted system files.
If related to Windows Update, reset the Windows Update cache using the Windows Update Troubleshooter.

issue: Invalid File Name or Directory Name
description: The file name, directory name, or volume label syntax is incorrect.
fix: Ensure the file or path name does not contain illegal characters (e.g., /, \, :, *, ?, ", <, >, |).
Try accessing the file from a shorter path or rename the file to remove special characters.

description: An operation timed out before it could complete, often related to Windows Update or network operations.
fix: Check your internet connection and retry the operation.
Restart the Windows Update service via Services (services.msc) and try again.

description: A COM object does not support the requested interface, typically encountered during software installation or COM registration.
fix: Re-register the affected COM component using 'regsvr32 <dll_name>.dll' in an elevated command prompt.
Reinstall the software that triggered the error.

description: The required class is not registered in the system registry, usually because a component or DLL is missing.
fix: Re-register the missing COM component with 'regsvr32' or reinstall the associated application.
Check Windows Event Viewer for additional details about the missing class.

description: A program attempted to read or write to a memory address that it is not authorized to access.
fix: Update the affected application to the latest version.
Disable Data Execution Prevention (DEP) exceptions for trusted applications via System Properties > Advanced > Performance.

description: The system could not find the requested object, commonly occurring during failed Windows updates.
fix: Run the Windows Update Troubleshooter from Settings > System > Troubleshoot.
Manually reset Windows Update components using the Windows Update Reset Script.

description: The system lacks sufficient resources (memory, handles, etc.) to complete the operation.
fix: Restart the system to free up resources.
Identify and close resource-heavy processes using Task Manager.

description: A DLL required by the application failed to initialize during startup.
fix: Run 'sfc /scannow' to repair any corrupted system DLLs.
Reinstall the application that is reporting the error.

description: A file or directory is corrupted and unreadable, often encountered during Windows installation or file operations.
fix: Run 'chkdsk /r' on the affected drive to repair file system errors.
If during Windows installation, check the installation media for errors or re-download the ISO.

description: A mismatch occurred in the Asynchronous Procedure Call (APC) disable count, typically caused by a kernel-mode driver or system service corrupting the APC state.
fix: Use Driver Verifier to identify the driver causing the APC state corruption and update or remove it.
Uninstall recently installed third-party antivirus or virtualization software and test for recurrence.

description: An invalid processor affinity set was specified for a thread, indicating a kernel scheduling error often tied to a buggy driver.
fix: Boot into Safe Mode and uninstall recently added drivers or system utilities.
Run 'sfc /scannow' to repair corrupted system files and restart.

description: The system ran out of Page Table Entries (PTEs), preventing memory mapping operations — commonly caused by heavy video memory usage or driver leaks.
fix: Update GPU and storage drivers to the latest manufacturer versions.
Reduce the number of simultaneously running graphics-intensive applications.

description: A process was terminated using an invalid method, often caused by a driver calling incorrect kernel termination routines.
fix: Use BlueScreenView to identify the faulting driver from the minidump and update it.
Run 'DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth' to repair the system image.

description: A kernel-mode component attempted to use an invalid process handle, indicating driver corruption or handle table tampering.
fix: Run Driver Verifier to isolate the offending driver and update it from the manufacturer's site.
Perform a clean boot to identify conflicting third-party services.

description: The kernel encountered an error while accessing a stack page, often caused by faulty RAM or disk I/O failures.
fix: Run Windows Memory Diagnostic or MemTest86 to check for faulty RAM modules.
Run 'chkdsk /r' to detect and repair disk errors affecting page file access.

issue: IRQL Not Less or Equal — Kernel (IRQL_NOT_LESS_OR_EQUAL_KERNEL)
description: A kernel-mode routine attempted to access paged memory at an elevated IRQL, typically from a driver bug.
fix: Identify the faulting driver using the minidump and update or roll it back.
Disable hardware acceleration in browsers and affected applications.

description: The kernel auto-boost lock acquisition mechanism encountered an invalid state, usually caused by a driver holding a lock too long.
fix: Update all storage and file system filter drivers to their latest versions.
Use Driver Verifier with low resources simulation to reproduce and isolate the fault.

description: The maximum number of wait context entries was exceeded, indicating a driver deadlock or resource leak.
fix: Update or remove recently installed third-party drivers, especially filter drivers.
Run 'verifier /standard /all' in Safe Mode to identify the problematic driver.

description: A mutex level number violation occurred, indicating a kernel synchronization object was corrupted by a driver.
fix: Boot into Safe Mode and uninstall recently added kernel-mode drivers.
Run 'sfc /scannow' and 'DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth' to repair system integrity.

description: The kernel could not find a valid user-mode context for the current thread, often caused by stack corruption.
fix: Test RAM with MemTest86 and replace faulty modules.
Update chipset and CPU microcode drivers from your motherboard manufacturer.

description: The kernel ran out of free stack pages, indicating a memory leak or excessive thread creation by a driver.
fix: Increase the system page file size via System Properties > Advanced > Virtual Memory.
Use Task Manager to identify processes creating excessive threads and update or remove them.

description: A resume operation was attempted on a thread that was not in a suspended state, pointing to a driver synchronization error.
fix: Use Driver Verifier to identify the driver issuing incorrect thread suspend/resume calls.
Uninstall recently installed security or monitoring software that hooks kernel threads.

description: The kernel received a trap with an unknown cause, typically caused by hardware instability or a severely corrupted driver.
fix: Reset BIOS/UEFI to default settings and disable any CPU overclocking.
Run extended RAM diagnostics with MemTest86 for at least 4 passes.

description: A worker thread returned at an invalid IRQL, corrupting the working set pool — usually from a buggy driver.
fix: Run Driver Verifier to catch the driver returning at the wrong IRQL and update it.
Perform a clean boot to isolate conflicting third-party kernel services.

description: A severe memory management error occurred, often caused by faulty RAM, corrupted page tables, or a driver corrupting memory.
fix: Run Windows Memory Diagnostic and MemTest86 to test all RAM modules.
Run 'sfc /scannow' and check for corrupted system files that may affect memory management.

description: A Page Frame Number (PFN) share count mismatch was detected, indicating memory corruption from faulty hardware or drivers.
fix: Reseat RAM modules and test with MemTest86 to rule out hardware faults.
Update storage and chipset drivers to prevent memory map corruption.

description: A PFN reference count mismatch occurred, meaning the kernel detected an inconsistency in page frame tracking — often hardware-related.
fix: Replace suspect RAM modules and run extended memory diagnostics.
Check motherboard BIOS for updated memory compatibility and flash if available.

description: A kernel APC was still pending when a thread exited, indicating a driver failed to properly flush APCs.
fix: Update all network and storage filter drivers to their latest versions.
Use Driver Verifier to isolate the driver leaking APCs.

description: The system quota manager detected an underflow condition, meaning a process released more quota than it held — typically a driver bug.
fix: Run 'sfc /scannow' to repair system file integrity.
Uninstall recently installed third-party drivers and test for recurrence.

description: A file system driver was found to be corrupt, causing instability in disk I/O operations.
fix: Update storage controller and file system filter drivers from the manufacturer.
Run 'chkdsk /f' and 'sfc /scannow' to repair file system and driver integrity.

description: An error occurred in the FAT file system driver, often caused by disk corruption or faulty storage controllers.
fix: Run 'chkdsk /r' on all FAT-formatted drives to repair file system errors.
Update storage controller drivers and check drive health with CrystalDiskInfo.

description: The CD-ROM file system driver encountered a critical error, typically from corrupted optical media or a failing disc drive.
fix: Update the optical disc drive firmware and motherboard chipset drivers.
Try different media or use a USB-based installation instead of optical media.

description: The redirector (RDR) file system driver encountered an error, commonly related to network file access problems.
fix: Update network adapter drivers and verify SMB protocol settings.
Check network connectivity and firewall rules affecting shared drive access.

description: The system detected a corrupted access token, often caused by security software or kernel-level hooking drivers.
fix: Uninstall third-party security suites and test with Windows Defender only.
Run 'sfc /scannow' and 'DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth' to repair system integrity.

description: The security system encountered a fatal error, typically from corrupted security subsystem files or incompatible security drivers.
fix: Boot into Safe Mode and uninstall recently added security or encryption software.
Run 'sfc /scannow' to repair security-related system files.

description: The kernel could not read a page of data from memory into the page file, often caused by failing storage drives or faulty RAM.
fix: Run 'chkdsk /r' to scan for and repair bad sectors on the system drive.
Test RAM with MemTest86 and check SSD/HDD health with CrystalDiskInfo.

description: A kernel stack switch occurred during a panic condition, typically caused by a stack overflow from a recursive driver call.
fix: Update the driver identified in the minidump to prevent recursive stack usage.
Increase kernel stack resources by disabling unnecessary filter drivers.

description: A Machine Check Architecture (MCA) bus error was detected, indicating a hardware fault on the system bus.
fix: Reseat all expansion cards and RAM modules to ensure proper contact.
Run motherboard diagnostic tools and check for failing hardware components.

description: The SCSI disk driver encountered a critical error, often from a failing SCSI controller or cable issues.
fix: Check and replace SCSI cables and verify proper termination of the SCSI chain.
Update the SCSI controller firmware and driver from the manufacturer.

description: An error occurred while setting the power state of a virtual hard disk device, commonly related to virtualization drivers.
fix: Update virtualization platform (Hyper-V, VirtualBox, VMware) to the latest version.
Check VHD file integrity and ensure the host drive has adequate free space.

description: The PCI bus driver encountered a critical error, often from a faulty PCI device or corrupted bus configuration.
fix: Reseat PCI/PCIe cards and update motherboard BIOS/UEFI to the latest version.
Remove non-essential PCIe cards one by one to identify the faulty device.

description: The CPU reported an internal error, indicating a hardware-level processor fault that may require replacement.
fix: Update motherboard BIOS to the latest version for CPU microcode patches.
Disable overclocking and run CPU stress tests to verify stability.

description: An unrecoverable machine check exception occurred, indicating a critical hardware failure in the CPU, cache, or memory subsystem.
fix: Run manufacturer hardware diagnostics for CPU and motherboard.
Check for BIOS updates addressing machine check exceptions and replace failing hardware.

description: A driver attempted to use more IRP stack locations than available, indicating a driver chain that is too deep or corrupted.
fix: Identify and remove unnecessary file system filter drivers using 'fltmc filters'.
Update all storage and antivirus filter drivers to their latest versions.

description: A device object reference count leak was detected, meaning a driver failed to properly release references — leading to resource exhaustion.
fix: Use Driver Verifier to identify the driver leaking device references and update it.
Restart the system periodically to mitigate resource exhaustion until the driver is fixed.

description: The floppy disk driver encountered a critical error, typically from failing floppy hardware or corrupted driver files.
fix: Disable the floppy drive controller in BIOS/UEFI if no floppy drive is present.
Update or uninstall the floppy disk driver from Device Manager.

description: The serial port driver encountered a critical error, often from a faulty serial device or IRQ conflict.
fix: Check for IRQ conflicts in Device Manager and reassign conflicting resources.
Update serial port (COM port) drivers and check physical cable connections.

description: The system failed to properly exit hibernation, often caused by driver power state inconsistencies or corrupted hibernation file.
fix: Disable hibernation with 'powercfg /hibernate off' and re-enable it to recreate the hibernation file.
Update all drivers, especially storage and chipset, to ensure proper power state transitions.

description: The system failed to enter hibernation, typically from insufficient disk space for the hibernation file or driver power issues.
fix: Ensure the system drive has free space equal to or greater than installed RAM.
Run 'powercfg /energy' to identify drivers preventing hibernation and update them.

description: The USB driver encountered a critical error, often from a faulty USB device, cable, or controller.
fix: Disconnect all USB devices and reconnect them one by one to identify the faulty device.
Update USB controller drivers and motherboard chipset drivers from the manufacturer.

description: An interrupt was not properly handled by the kernel, indicating a driver or hardware interrupt routing problem.
fix: Update all device drivers and check for IRQ conflicts in Device Manager.
Reset BIOS/UEFI interrupt settings to default and test for stability.

description: The system detected a mismatch in multiprocessor configuration, often from mismatched CPU steppings or BIOS issues.
fix: Update motherboard BIOS to the latest version for proper multiprocessor support.
Ensure all CPUs (in multi-socket systems) are of the same model and stepping.

description: A registry filtering exception occurred, typically caused by a registry filter driver failing to properly handle a registry operation.
fix: Uninstall third-party registry cleaners or system optimizers that install registry filter drivers.
Run 'sfc /scannow' to repair corrupted registry-related system files.

description: The Multiple UNC Provider (MUP) file system driver encountered an error, commonly related to network path resolution failures.
fix: Update network redirector and network adapter drivers.
Check DNS and network configuration to ensure UNC paths resolve correctly.

description: The must-succeed pool was empty, meaning the kernel could not allocate critical memory — often from severe memory exhaustion.
fix: Increase physical RAM or reduce the number of running applications and services.
Increase the page file size and check for memory leaks using Task Manager.

description: The ATAPI (IDE/SATA) disk driver encountered a critical error, often from failing storage hardware or corrupted drivers.
fix: Replace SATA cables and update storage controller drivers from the motherboard manufacturer.
Check drive health with CrystalDiskInfo and run 'chkdsk /r' on affected drives.

description: The system could not find a device that was expected to be present, often from a removed or failed hardware component.
fix: Reseat the device and check its power and data connections.
Update the device driver and check Device Manager for disabled or failed devices.

description: Multiple uncorrected I/O errors were detected, indicating widespread storage or controller failure.
fix: Back up data immediately and replace the failing storage drive.
Update storage controller firmware and check SATA/NVMe connections.

description: The system lacks sufficient resources (memory, handles, pools) to complete the operation, often from a resource leak.
fix: Restart the system and monitor resource usage in Task Manager over time.
Use Poolmon to identify the kernel pool tag leaking memory and update the associated driver.

description: A deregistered driver was found to be corrupt, indicating the driver was improperly unloaded or its code was overwritten.
fix: Use Driver Verifier to identify the corrupt driver and reinstall it from the manufacturer.
Run 'sfc /scannow' to repair corrupted system driver files.

description: The kernel detected an inconsistent stack state, often from a driver corrupting the kernel stack or a hardware memory fault.
fix: Run MemTest86 to test for faulty RAM that may be corrupting kernel stacks.
Update all kernel-mode drivers and disable overclocking in BIOS.

description: A cancel state was found in an APC, indicating a driver improperly handled I/O cancellation within an APC routine.
fix: Update the driver identified in the minidump that mishandled the APC cancel.
Use Driver Verifier to reproduce the issue and isolate the faulty driver.

description: The hardware profile was found to be damaged or corrupted, preventing proper device enumeration.
fix: Boot into Safe Mode and restore the default hardware profile.
Run 'sfc /scannow' and check Device Manager for devices with error states.

description: A driver attempted to attach to a process in an invalid context, indicating improper use of kernel process attachment APIs.
fix: Identify the driver using KeStackAttachProcess incorrectly via the minidump and update it.
Uninstall third-party monitoring or security software that hooks process operations.

description: The USB host controller driver encountered a critical error, often from a power surge on a USB port or faulty USB hardware.
fix: Disconnect all USB devices and update USB host controller drivers.
Check for USB power surge notifications and replace damaged USB cables or hubs.

description: A fatal hardware error was detected that the system could not correct, indicating imminent hardware failure.
fix: Run comprehensive hardware diagnostics for CPU, RAM, and storage.
Replace the failing hardware component identified by diagnostics.

description: The system ran out of free physical memory pages, causing a critical memory exhaustion crash.
fix: Increase physical RAM or reduce memory-intensive applications.
Use RAMMap to identify memory consumption patterns and close leaking processes.

description: The PFN (Page Frame Number) list was found to be corrupted, typically caused by faulty RAM or a driver passing invalid memory descriptors.
fix: Run MemTest86 for at least 4 passes to identify faulty RAM modules.
Update all drivers and run 'sfc /scannow' to repair system file corruption.

description: The Network Driver Interface Specification (NDIS) layer encountered an internal error, typically from a faulty network adapter driver.
fix: Update the network adapter driver from the manufacturer's website.
Reset the network stack with 'netsh winsock reset' and 'netsh int ip reset' in an elevated prompt.

description: A severe registry error occurred, often from corrupted registry hive files or a failing storage drive holding the registry.
fix: Run 'chkdsk /r' to repair disk errors affecting the registry.
Boot from installation media and use the Recovery Environment to restore the registry from backup.

description: A mailslot (inter-process communication mechanism) was found to be corrupted, typically from a driver or service mishandling IPC.
fix: Run 'sfc /scannow' to repair corrupted system IPC components.
Restart the affected service or reboot to reset mailslot state.

description: The Multiple UNC Provider received an invalid request, often from a corrupted network redirector or DNS resolution failure.
fix: Update the network redirector driver and verify DNS settings.
Flush DNS with 'ipconfig /flushdns' and reset the network stack.

description: The SMB (Server Message Block) protocol encountered a critical error, often from corrupted SMB configuration or network driver issues.
fix: Update network adapter drivers and verify SMB protocol settings in Windows Features.
Run 'sfc /scannow' and check Event Viewer for SMB-related service errors.

description: A data coherency error was detected, meaning cached and stored data were inconsistent — often from CPU cache or RAM faults.
fix: Disable CPU cache overclocking and reset BIOS to defaults.
Run CPU and memory diagnostics to identify failing hardware.

description: A kernel buffer was found to be in an inconsistent state, typically from a driver overwriting buffer boundaries.
fix: Use Driver Verifier with special pool to catch the driver corrupting kernel buffers.
Update the faulting driver identified in the minidump.

description: An NDIS miniport driver encountered a critical error, commonly from a faulty or outdated network adapter driver.
fix: Download and install the latest network adapter driver from the manufacturer.
Disable and re-enable the network adapter in Device Manager to reset its state.

description: An internal error occurred in the file system disk driver, often from corrupted partition tables or failing storage hardware.
fix: Run 'chkdsk /r' and verify partition integrity with 'diskpart'.
Back up data and replace the storage drive if SMART indicates failure.

description: The Pinball (HPFS) file system driver encountered an error, typically from corrupted disk structures or incompatible drivers.
fix: Run 'chkdsk /f' on the affected HPFS volume if accessible.
Update file system drivers and convert the volume to NTFS if compatibility is not required.

description: A critical system service failed to initialize during startup, often from a corrupted driver or missing system file.
fix: Boot from Windows installation media and run Startup Repair.
Use the Recovery Environment to run 'sfc /scannow' and restore missing system files.

description: A critical error occurred during Windows setup, typically from corrupted installation media or incompatible hardware.
fix: Re-download the Windows ISO using the Media Creation Tool and recreate installation media.
Disconnect non-essential hardware during setup and verify minimum system requirements.

description: The Hardware Abstraction Layer (HAL) failed to allocate required memory during initialization, often from BIOS misconfiguration.
fix: Update motherboard BIOS/UEFI to the latest version.
Reseat RAM modules and verify memory configuration matches motherboard specifications.

description: The system detected a processor that is not supported by the current Windows version or BIOS, preventing boot.
fix: Update motherboard BIOS to add support for the installed CPU model.
Verify the CPU is on the Windows compatibility list for your installed version.

description: A critical system process failed to initialize properly, often from corrupted system files or incompatible drivers.
fix: Boot into Safe Mode and run 'sfc /scannow' followed by 'DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth'.
Uninstall recently added drivers or updates that may have caused the initialization failure.

description: The security subsystem failed to initialize, typically from corrupted security-related system files or registry entries.
fix: Boot from installation media and run Startup Repair.
Run 'sfc /scannow' from the Recovery Environment to repair security subsystem files.

description: A worker pool integrity check failed, indicating memory corruption in the kernel worker thread pool — usually from a buggy driver.
fix: Run Driver Verifier to identify the driver corrupting worker pool memory.
Update all third-party kernel-mode drivers to their latest versions.

description: The Hardware Abstraction Layer failed to initialize, often from BIOS misconfiguration or incompatible hardware.
fix: Reset BIOS/UEFI to default settings and update to the latest firmware version.
Remove non-essential hardware and test boot with minimal configuration.

description: The kernel object manager failed to initialize, typically from corrupted system files or severe registry corruption.
fix: Boot from installation media and run Startup Repair.
Use the Recovery Environment to restore the registry from a known-good backup.

description: The security kernel subsystem failed during early initialization, often from corrupted security tokens or system files.
fix: Run 'sfc /scannow' from the Recovery Environment to repair security files.
Perform an in-place upgrade repair using Windows installation media.

description: An error occurred while handling symbolic links during kernel initialization, often from corrupted file system metadata.
fix: Run 'chkdsk /r' to repair file system metadata and symbolic link structures.
Boot from installation media and run Startup Repair if the system is unbootable.

description: The memory manager failed during early kernel initialization, typically from incompatible or faulty RAM.
fix: Reseat RAM modules and test each stick individually with MemTest86.
Update BIOS memory timing settings and verify RAM compatibility with the motherboard.

description: The kernel cache manager failed to initialize, often from corrupted cache-related system files or storage driver issues.
fix: Update storage controller drivers and run 'sfc /scannow' to repair system files.
Boot into Safe Mode and clear the system cache using 'Disk Cleanup'.

description: The configuration manager (registry subsystem) failed to initialize, typically from corrupted registry hive files.
fix: Boot from installation media and restore the registry from backup using the Recovery Environment.
Run 'chkdsk /r' to repair disk errors that may have corrupted the registry.

description: The file system failed to initialize during kernel startup, often from corrupted boot files or storage driver issues.
fix: Boot from installation media and run Startup Repair to fix boot file corruption.
Update storage controller drivers and verify boot drive health.

description: The I/O manager failed to initialize during kernel startup, typically from corrupted I/O system files or storage controller failure.
fix: Boot from installation media and run 'sfc /scannow' from the Recovery Environment.
Check storage controller configuration in BIOS and update relevant drivers.

description: The Local Procedure Call (LPC) subsystem failed to initialize, often from corrupted system files or security token issues.
fix: Run 'sfc /scannow' from the Recovery Environment to repair LPC-related system files.
Perform an in-place upgrade repair if the system remains unbootable.

description: The process manager failed during kernel initialization, typically from corrupted system files or registry issues.
fix: Boot from installation media and run Startup Repair.
Restore the system registry from a backup using the Recovery Environment.

description: The reference monitor (security reference monitor) failed to initialize, often from corrupted security-related system files.
fix: Run 'sfc /scannow' and 'DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth' from the Recovery Environment.
Perform a clean reinstall of Windows if system repair tools cannot fix the corruption.

description: The first session manager failed to initialize, typically from corrupted session-related system files or memory issues.
fix: Boot into Safe Mode and run 'sfc /scannow' to repair system files.
Test RAM with MemTest86 and replace faulty modules if detected.

description: The second session initialization failed, often from corrupted session manager files or driver conflicts during startup.
fix: Boot from installation media and run Startup Repair.
Uninstall recently added drivers or software that may conflict with session initialization.

description: The third session initialization failed, typically from corrupted system files or severe registry corruption.
fix: Run 'sfc /scannow' from the Recovery Environment and attempt boot.
Perform an in-place upgrade repair using the Media Creation Tool.

description: The fourth session initialization failed, often from memory exhaustion or corrupted session configuration data.
fix: Increase the page file size and verify adequate free disk space on the system drive.
Run 'sfc /scannow' and 'chkdsk /r' from the Recovery Environment.

description: The fifth session initialization failed, typically from corrupted system files or incompatible kernel-mode drivers.
fix: Boot into Safe Mode and uninstall recently added kernel-mode drivers.
Run 'sfc /scannow' and 'DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth' to repair system integrity.

description: The configuration manager failed to process the registry configuration list, often from severe registry hive corruption.
fix: Boot from installation media and restore the registry from a known-good backup.
Run 'chkdsk /r' to repair disk errors that may have corrupted registry hives.

description: The system configuration information is corrupt or incorrect, typically from corrupted registry entries or a failed Windows update.
fix: Boot from installation media, go to Troubleshoot > Advanced Options > Startup Settings, and restart.
Use the Recovery Environment to restore the registry from backup or perform a System Restore.

description: The system could not assign a device to the requested operation, often from resource conflicts or a missing device driver.
fix: Check Device Manager for devices with error states and update their drivers.
Resolve IRQ or I/O port conflicts by reassigning resources in BIOS/UEFI.

description: A process failed to release locked memory pages, indicating a driver memory leak — the system tracks the offending driver tag.
fix: Use Poolmon to identify the pool tag leaking locked pages and update the associated driver.
Run Driver Verifier to force the leaking driver into revealing itself.

description: The kernel could not read a requested page of kernel stack data from the page file, often from storage drive failures or faulty RAM.
fix: Run 'chkdsk /r' to check for and repair bad sectors on the system drive.
Test RAM with MemTest86 and check drive SMART health with CrystalDiskInfo.

description: An exception occurred during phase 0 of kernel initialization, typically from corrupted system files or incompatible hardware.
fix: Boot from installation media and run Startup Repair.
Update BIOS/UEFI to the latest version and verify hardware compatibility.

description: The Hardware Abstraction Layer (HAL) does not match the system type, often from an incorrect Windows installation or BIOS change.
fix: Perform a clean reinstall of Windows with the correct installation media for your system.
Reset BIOS/UEFI to default settings and verify the system type matches the installed Windows version.

description: The NDIS network driver encountered a critical error, typically from a faulty network adapter driver or hardware.
fix: Update the network adapter driver from the manufacturer's website.
Replace the network adapter if hardware diagnostics indicate failure.

description: The system does not have enough physical memory to start Windows, preventing boot entirely.
fix: Install additional RAM to meet the minimum requirement for your Windows version.
Reseat existing RAM modules and verify they are properly recognized in BIOS.

description: A kernel spinlock initialization failed, indicating a kernel synchronization object was corrupted — usually from a driver bug.
fix: Use Driver Verifier to identify the driver corrupting spinlock objects.
Update all kernel-mode drivers and run 'sfc /scannow' to repair system files.

description: The Distributed File System (DFS) driver encountered a critical error, often from corrupted DFS configuration or network issues.
fix: Verify DFS configuration and namespace settings in the DFS Management console.
Update network adapter drivers and check connectivity to DFS servers.

description: A critical error occurred during Windows setup, typically from corrupted installation files or incompatible hardware.
fix: Re-download the Windows ISO and recreate installation media with the Media Creation Tool.
Disconnect all non-essential USB devices and verify hardware meets minimum requirements.

description: The Master Boot Record (MBR) checksum did not match, indicating corruption of the boot sector — often from malware or disk errors.
fix: Boot from installation media and run 'bootrec /fixmbr' and 'bootrec /fixboot' in the Recovery Environment.
Run a full system malware scan with Windows Defender or a trusted third-party tool.

description: A kernel-mode exception was not handled by any exception handler, typically from a faulty driver or corrupted memory.
fix: Use BlueScreenView to identify the faulting driver from the minidump and update it.
Run 'sfc /scannow' and MemTest86 to check for system file and RAM corruption.

description: A device or driver did not complete a power state transition within the allotted time, causing a watchdog timeout.
fix: Update all drivers, especially those for USB, network, and storage devices.
Disable Fast Startup in Power Options and test if the issue recurs.

description: The system was overloaded with Deferred Procedure Calls (DPCs) in worker threads, typically from a driver scheduling excessive DPCs.
fix: Use xperf or Windows Performance Analyzer to identify the driver generating excessive DPCs.
Update or remove the offending driver identified through DPC tracing.

description: A kernel-mode component used an invalid handle, indicating handle table corruption or a driver bug.
fix: Run Driver Verifier to identify the driver using invalid kernel handles.
Update all kernel-mode drivers and run 'sfc /scannow' to repair system integrity.

description: A thread exited while still holding a kernel stack lock, indicating a driver failed to release a lock before thread termination.
fix: Identify the faulting driver from the minidump and update it.
Use Driver Verifier to reproduce the issue and isolate the driver leaking stack locks.

description: A work queue item was found to be invalid, indicating a driver queued a corrupted or already-freed work item.
fix: Use Driver Verifier to identify the driver queuing invalid work items and update it.
Uninstall recently added third-party drivers and test for recurrence.

description: The system encountered a bound image that is not supported, often from corrupted executable files or incompatible driver binaries.
fix: Run 'sfc /scannow' to repair corrupted system executables.
Reinstall the application or driver that triggered the error.

description: The evaluation period for this Windows installation has expired, causing the system to stop booting normally.

If this is an evaluation build, perform a clean install with a licensed version of Windows.

description: The kernel detected an invalid memory region or segment, typically from a driver corrupting virtual memory descriptors.
fix: Run Driver Verifier to identify the driver corrupting memory regions and update it.
Test RAM with MemTest86 to rule out hardware memory faults.

description: The system detected a Windows licensing violation, often from a tampered or counterfeit installation.
fix: Run the Windows Activation Troubleshooter and enter a valid product key.
Perform a clean reinstall of Windows using genuine installation media.

description: A fatal machine check exception occurred, indicating a hardware-level error in the CPU, cache, or bus — often from overclocking or failing hardware.
fix: Reset all BIOS overclocking settings to defaults and test for stability.
Run CPU and motherboard diagnostics; replace failing hardware components.

description: The user-mode health monitor detected a critical user-mode process failure, often from a corrupted system service or driver.
fix: Run 'sfc /scannow' and 'DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth' to repair system files.
Check Event Viewer for the specific user-mode process that failed and reinstall related software.

description: The power policy manager encountered an internal error, typically from a driver failing to handle power state transitions correctly.
fix: Update all drivers, especially display, storage, and chipset drivers.
Run 'powercfg /energy' to identify power management issues and apply recommended fixes.

description: A PCI bus error was detected, often from a faulty PCIe device, corrupted BIOS configuration, or motherboard hardware fault.
fix: Reseat all PCIe cards and update motherboard BIOS to the latest version.
Remove PCIe cards one by one to identify the faulty device.

description: The memory image was found to be corrupted, typically from faulty RAM or a driver overwriting memory regions it does not own.
fix: Run MemTest86 for at least 4 passes to identify faulty RAM modules.
Use Driver Verifier with special pool to catch the driver corrupting memory.

description: The ACPI (Advanced Configuration and Power Interface) driver encountered an internal error, often from BIOS power management bugs.

Update chipset drivers and disable unnecessary ACPI power management features in BIOS.

description: A critical file system error occurred in the CNSS (Certification Network Security System) driver, often from corrupted network security configuration.

Reset network configuration with 'netsh winsock reset' and 'netsh int ip reset'.

description: The system BIOS is not fully ACPI compliant, causing the kernel to halt during power management initialization.

Run 'sfc /scannow' and update all kernel-mode drivers.

Verify SATA mode (AHCI/RAID) in BIOS matches the storage controller driver configuration.

description: A kernel hardlock was detected, meaning the system hung at a high IRQL with interrupts disabled — often from a driver deadlock.

Reseat the graphics card and check GPU power connections if applicable.

description: A thread attempted to switch from a DPC (Deferred Procedure Call) routine, which is not allowed — indicating a driver bug.

Update the faulting driver identified in the minidump.

description: The system chipset reported a critical error, often from failing motherboard components or incompatible hardware.

Check motherboard capacitors and VRM components for physical damage.

description: A session was found to have no viewports, indicating a display subsystem initialization failure — often from GPU driver issues.

Update the display driver from the GPU manufacturer's website.

Disable network boot in BIOS/UEFI if PXE boot is not required.

description: A driver attempted to write to read-only memory, indicating a driver bug or memory corruption.

Run 'sfc /scannow' and MemTest86 to check for system file and RAM corruption.

Continue running Driver Verifier until all corrupting drivers are identified and fixed.

Update the identified driver from the manufacturer's website.
